summary: Of the 20 prior UAT findings, 17 are confirmed fixed, 1 has regressed (UAT-FN-12: seed 10 now reports 0 journal entries instead of 8; idempotency guard over-fires), and 2 were not re-tested in this follow-up (UAT-FN-06 profile init TTY handling, UAT-FN-08 vector dim consistency). 1 minor new finding: UAT-FN-21, a Node DeprecationWarning emitted at the end of self-test. All four blocker-class findings (FN-01, FN-02, FN-03, FN-04) are fixed and the README quickstart now works end-to-end (7/7 commands).
persona: First-time EdgeWell user; Node.js developer; 20-min budget

findings.title: README quickstart does not work - node bin/edgewell.js <cmd> fails with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND
findings.actual: Help screen prints cleanly via the bin/edgewell.js shim

findings.title: README's exact RAG example query returns no matches
findings.repro: rag ingest data/sample_health_notes.txt then rag search "how much water should I drink"
findings.actual: Returns 5 matches containing the hydration line

findings.title: README install command `npm install` fails; project requires pnpm
findings.actual: README now opens with a pnpm disclaimer and shows `pnpm install`

findings.title: `profile set --key value` corrupts profile.json
findings.repro: node bin/edgewell.js profile set --name alex --goal sleep
findings.expected: Reject with `'--name' looks like a flag, not a profile key`
findings.actual: Rejects with the exact spec message; profile.json is not modified; exit 2

findings.title: `--version` flag silently ignored
findings.actual: Both print `edgewell v3.0.0`

findings.title: `profile init` hangs silently when stdin is not a TTY
findings.actual: n/a
findings.suggestedFix: If still present, detect !process.stdin.isTTY and either write a default profile or print a clear error and exit non-zero.

findings.title: `doctor` falsely reports `profile readable name=friend` when profile.json is missing
findings.expected: FAIL profile readable with `no profile on disk`
findings.actual: FAIL profile readable  no profile on disk - run `edgewell profile init`

findings.title: Inconsistent vector dimensions: doctor says 64, vector stats says 128
findings.actual: Note: F10 doctor output showed `dim=128`, so the original mismatch may have been fixed, but a clean diff is not produced here.
findings.suggestedFix: If still present, share a single source of truth for vector dim.

findings.title: `redact` command cannot be invoked from the CLI
findings.repro: node bin/edgewell.js redact "Contact me at john@example.com"
findings.actual: Contact me at [REDACTED_EMAIL]

findings.title: `self-test` reports 'self-test passed' while running 0 tests
findings.expected: Run real tests and report pass/fail
findings.actual: Runs 427 tests, all pass, prints real counts
findings.suggestedFix: Already fixed in f027422 (but see UAT-FN-21 for the DeprecationWarning)

findings.title: `version-history` says 'not a git checkout or CHANGELOG not tracked'
findings.actual: Lists 0619e33 v3.0.0 commit and d26c55c v2.0.0

findings.title: `seed N` journal undercount is now a zero count
findings.actual: seeded 0 journal entries and 10 expenses. A second `seed 10` call returns 0/0. The unit test `seed appends the requested number of journal entries` passes inside self-test, so the regression is masked when journal.jsonl already contains 10000 entries from prior seeds. Most likely cause: the new idempotency guard (`seed is idempotent`) is over-firing on the first call too.
findings.suggestedFix: Scope the idempotency check to a content hash of the (count, last-seed-ts) pair, not a global "have we seeded N before" flag. Add an integration test that runs `seed 10` against a fresh data/ dir and asserts journals===10 && expenses===10.

findings.title: Unknown subcommand dumps the entire 110-line help screen
findings.actual: unknown command: frobnicate\nrun `edgewell help` to see the full command list\nexit 2

findings.title: Raw `ENOENT` / `EISDIR` errors leak from `rag ingest`
findings.repro: node bin/edgewell.js rag ingest /tmp/nope.txt and node bin/edgewell.js rag ingest data
findings.expected: file not found: <path> and <path> is a directory, not a file
findings.actual: Both friendly messages as specified

findings.title: `seed` accepts arbitrarily large N with no cap
findings.actual: seed count 1000000 exceeds cap of 100000 (use a smaller number, or run multiple times if you really need more)

findings.title: `chat` only processes one piped input then exits
findings.actual: Both lines processed; exit closes the loop cleanly

findings.title: `expense add 250` (no category) silently defaults to 'other'
findings.actual: usage: edgewell expense add <amount> <category> (category is required; "other" is no longer auto-filled) / exit 2

findings.title: `version` and `version-history` disagree on what counts as a release
findings.actual: version-history now lists the v3.0.0 commit, consistent with `edgewell version`

findings.title: Help screen has a 138-command wall of name-only 'additional commands'
findings.repro: node bin/edgewell.js help (look at the More commands line)
findings.actual: More commands: (138 additional - run `edgewell command-list` for the full list)\n  journal-* (43)  tag-* (38)  expenses-* (30)  snapshot-* (6)  monthly-* (2)  rag-* (2)  release-* (2)  sample-* (2)\n  … and 11 more stems

findings.title: `where` shows profile path even when the file does not exist
findings.actual: profile: /…/data/profile.json  (not yet created)

findings.title: self-test emits DeprecationWarning: Passing args to a child process with shell option true
findings.expected: Clean exit with no deprecation warnings
findings.actual: (node:NNNNN) [DEP0190] DeprecationWarning: Passing args to a child process with shell option true can lead to security vulnerabilities, as the arguments are not escaped, only concatenated.
findings.suggestedFix: Switch the self-test child-process invocation to `shell: false` with an explicit `argv: ['node', '--import', 'tsx', '--test', ...]` so the arguments are passed as an array and Node stops emitting DEP0190.
